title: Summer Is Ended.
content:
To think that this meaningless thing was ever a rose
    Scentless, colorless, this!
    Will it ever be thus (who knows?)
    Thus with our bliss,
    If we wait till the close?

    Though we care not to wait for the end, there comes the end
    Sooner, later, at last,
    Which nothing can mar, nothing mend:
    An end locked fast,
    Bent we cannot re-bend.
meaning: About: Christina Georgina Rossetti was a British poet, who wrote a variety of romantic, devotional, and children's poems. She is best known for her long poem Goblin Market, her love poem "Remember", and for her Christmas poem "In the Bleak Midwinter".
